title: Error target is unknown on docker pull for image pushed with buildah
body:
**Description**

I build an image with *buildah bud* then i push it to a private registry with a *buildah push* command.

The image is visible on registry GUI, and i can list the tag with the endpoint : https://myregistry/v2/MY_IMAGE_NAME/tags/list.

When i try a *docker pull* command i have this message : ` Error response from daemon: target is unknown`

**Note** : It's possible to pull the image with *buildah from* but not with *docker pull*

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build an image with *buildah bud*...
2. Push an image to a private registry (nexus3) with `buildah push --creds=${CRED} MY_IMAGE_ID docker://MY_IMAGE_NAME_AND_TAG`
3. Docker login and Pull an image from Docker version 17.03.1-ce, build c6d412e daemon : `docker pull MY_IMAGE_TAG`


**Describe the results you received:**

Error from daemon docker logs :
```
level=debug msg="Calling POST /v1.27/images/create?fromImage=MY_IMAGE_NAME_AND_TAG"
level=debug msg="hostDir: /etc/docker/certs.d/MYREGISTRY"
level=debug msg="hostDir: /etc/docker/certs.d/MYREGISTRY"
level=debug msg="Trying to pull MY_IMAGE_NAME_AND_TAG from https://MYREGISTRY v2"
level=debug msg="Calling GET /v1.30/services"
level=debug msg="Calling GET /v1.30/tasks"
level=error msg="Error trying v2 registry: target is unknown"
level=error msg="Attempting next endpoint for pull after error: target is unknown"
level=debug msg="Skipping v1 endpoint https://MYREGISTRY because v2 registry was detected"
 level=error msg="Handler for POST /v1.27/images/create returned error: target is unknown"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Get the image pulled.


**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    82529cd
Built:         Fri Apr  6 10:15:58 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
CentOS Linux release 7.3.1611 (Core) 
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="7 (Core)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="7"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 7 (Core)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:7"
HOME_URL="https://www.centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"

CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-7"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="centos"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7"

CentOS Linux release 7.3.1611 (Core) 
CentOS Linux release 7.3.1611 (Core) 
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux jenkins-slave-d0dlw-t2n6l 3.10.0-693.11.6.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Dec 28 14:23:39 EST 2017 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body: Random googling points at https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/30083 : either the registry is returning an invalid `Content-Type`, or maybe the registry supports OCI, buildah has created an OCI image, and Docker does not recognize it.

@rhatdan, no ideas at the moment, we do this as part of our baseline tests without an error so far.  I'm not sure, but I think the most we've tested against is Docker 1.16.  I wonder if there's another change in Docker 1.17 that's not quite as anticipated.  I'll try to duplicate.

@gregoryboue I wasn't able to duplicate this on my test vm.  I was using Fedora 28 and Docker/Buildah versions in my output below.

A couple of tweaks to your environment to try and narrow the problem down.  Can you upgrade Docker-ce?  I used a slightly newer version. My other thought was perhaps there's something with the private registry that's going on.  Can you try testing against docker.io (Docker Hub) or quay.io and see if that works for you?

If that's still no joy for you, I'll see if I can set up a private repository outside of Docker Hub and see if it's related there.

HTH's!

Yes the  private registry chose by my company for CI environment is nexus sonatype 3
It seems to work with portus but the image is notre display from Portus GUI

**Edit 13/04/18** :
The docker pull with `Docker version 1.11.0, build 4dc5990` works

@gregoryboue I've not used nexus3 before myself, will see if I can dig one up to try.  In the mean time, were you able to try a newer version of Docker-ce to see if it's still reproducing?  Also if you push the image initially using Docker 1.11.0, do you get the same error when you try to pull it using Docker version 17.03.1-ce?

@TomSweeneyRedHat 

I did the following : 

1. buildah bud...
2. buildah push MY_IMAGE
3. (docker 1.11.0) : docker pull
4. (docker 1.11.0) : docker tag
5. (docker 1.11.0) : docker push
6. (docker 17.03.1-ce) : docker pull

the docker pull works, i haven't environment with a newer version of docker-ce i will try to get one.

Thanks

@TomSweeneyRedHat I had news elements.

When i browse the assets which correspond to the manifests image file in Nexus 3 I can see the following :

The manifest of an image pushed by docker push command looks like this : 

```
{
   "schemaVersion": 2,
   "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
   "config": {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json",
      "size": 6410,
      "digest": "sha256:23d1c505e127930cae6d0540a6c8ecfd7f59fc1c674f86343c9b54233e47ea23"
   },
   "layers": [
      {
         "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip",
         "size": 52608519,
         "digest": "sha256:f2b6b4884fc8b2f1fcef843f92f7c82c9c149df85ac77e5f0de7a342ae442412"
      },...
```

The manifest of an image pushed by buildah push command looks like this : 

```
{  
   "schemaVersion":2,
   "config":{  
      "mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json",
      "digest":"sha256:23d1c505e127930cae6d0540a6c8ecfd7f59fc1c674f86343c9b54233e47ea23",
      "size":6410
   },
   "layers":[  
      {  
         "mediaType":"application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar",
         "digest":"sha256:f2b6b4884fc8b2f1fcef843f92f7c82c9c149df85ac77e5f0de7a342ae442412",
         "size":52608519
      },
```

The main difference is the missing mediaType attribute at the top level of the json object, I think this is responsible for the docker pull error.

Is this helps you ?

Thanks

> The main difference is the missing mediaType attribute at the top level of the json object, I think this is responsible for the docker pull error.

Yeah, this confirms my guess:
> either the registry is returning an invalid Content-Type, or maybe the registry supports OCI, buildah has created an OCI image, and Docker does not recognize it.

the two manifests differ in that one is Docker schema2 and the other is OCI.

It would still be useful to verify what `Content-Type` header is returned when fetching the manifest, to determine whether the bug is in the registry, the client, or both.  https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/30083 suggests that the client’s behavior has supposedly been fixed since.

(See #505 for a somewhat related discussion, but focused on docker/distribution; the registry in this case is clearly behaving differently.)

@mtrmac the content-type header returned has the following value : 
**application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json**

It should be **application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json** ?

Is the problem come from Nexus registry or from buildah ?

> @mtrmac the content-type header returned has the following value :
application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json

That’s definitely not correct.

> It should be application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json

Yes.

> Is the problem come from Nexus registry or from buildah ?

For _this particular subproblem_, I’d bet on the registry; the way to tell for sure would be to see the `Content-Type` set by `buildah` when uploading the manifest.

---

It’s not just returning the correct `Content-Type`, though: if Docker asks for a manifest and specifies that it does not support OCI, and the registry only has an OCI manifest, it must on-the-fly convert it into one of the supported formats.  If the registry can’t even recognize the OCI format enough to return the right `Content-Type` (or to reject the input if `buildah` were sending an invalid `Content-Type`), it’s highly unlikely that the conversion code exists.

I think that would be something to talk about with the vendor

> > It should be application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json

> Yes.

For the manifest, `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json` actually.

@mtrmac ok I understand.

This is the request when buildah push the manifest :

```
PUT //docker-XXXXX/v2/test/build-docker/manifests/0.4-SNAPSHOT HTTP/1.1
User-Agent: Go-http-client/1.1
Content-Length: 496
Authorization: Bearer DockerToken.48158b78-72a0-3bb7-ba67-b742XXXXX24
Content-Type: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Connection: close
Host: docker-XXXXX
X-Forwarded-Host: docker-XXXXX
X-Forwarded-Port: 443
X-Forwarded-Proto: https
Forwarded: for=1XXXXX;host=docker-XXXXX;proto=https
X-Forwarded-For: 1XXXXX
```

And the response From Nexus : 

```
201 null HTTP/1.1
Connection: close
Date: Wed, 25 Apr 2018 11:11:28 GMT
Server: Nexus/3.6.0-02 (OSS)
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Last-Modified: Wed, 25 Apr 2018 11:11:28 GMT
Docker-Content-Digest: sha256:a4869aba816cb490482be506ad0eade677f7597968a6ac2df218fb95a3f3a47a
Content-Type: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json
Content-Length: 496
```

So it means that the content type application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json is not managed by the registry ?
@mtrmac I opened an issue on Sonatype Nexus JIRA, and yes the --format docker solve the problem so I will use this workaround
Ok I don't see this as an issue with buildah so I am closing.  Reopen if you disagree.
